All right, welcome back here. I got to get my energy level back up. This will mellow downstairs. It was important for that particular exercise to be in a more relaxed state and to slowly build up the energy level. Listen, remember, the key here is that when you change the way you look at things, the things that you look at change. I don't know who first said that, but it's really profound and it's true. You know, it's not so much that your memories change, right? We all have our memories. It's what we do to give meaning to those memories. What words do we apply to a situation that causes us to view it in a certain light? And as a result of that, either we developed a limiting belief or an empowering belief. In other words, I could have gone all the way back to kindergarten with Mrs. Roskin in the bowl of fruit. And what if she would have said, oh, Jordan, you know, what you really need is, I need to show you exactly how to do this. You have so much talent. Once I show you what to do, you'll just love this and be so good at it. Just imagine if she would have said that. Maybe she'd have learned a bit though on such natural talent. Imagine if the experience would have had a meaning that, oh, you know, oh, guess what? Maybe teach you a strategy for drawing. And she would have taken a whole different tact and I probably would have said, oh, well, show me how to draw. Like that guy does on TV at the Afro and this is your world, right? And I could have become proficient at art. Yeah, maybe I'm not gonna be the next Van Gogh or Rembrandt, but the point is I wouldn't have had this limiting belief about taking action, because you know, I'm a good at it, why should I wanna do it? And that's how it all starts, right? In addition, one more takeaway here, just remember, I briefly mentioned this. I said, you know, we don't have spyware. We're not built with like, you know, antivirus software, meaning people can insert limiting beliefs in you by saying things to you. And it's like, you can't stand vigil there unless you're consciously aware that when people say certain things to you, they are inserting limiting beliefs in you. You know, my wife and I would be kid around with you, I said, oh, you're trying to insert limiting beliefs, maybe we actually kid around when we say something that might be skewed as negative, that, you know, wait, it's just what someone else's opinion is. It's not a fact of life, all it is is their belief. And when you hear something, as you're not really aware of that, it could slip through the conscious, you know, there's no spyware into your unconscious mind, you apply the wrong meaning and bam, you have the seed of a limiting belief, and then that cycle, reinforce it, reinforce it, reinforce it. And the rest, as they say, is history. So remember, we don't have spyware, so look very closely at the things that people are saying, you know, the experiences that you're taking in, listen, watch, learn, but don't give the meaning that maybe other people want to apply something, choose your own meaning and try to choose the empowering one. At some level, it always goes with the inner world and the outer world, the inner world is all intertwined and then it affects the outer world. Like for instance, we tend to draw negative meanings when we're in disempowered states. So you need to be aware of your state as well. You know, like when you do something and the result is negative, you tend to go into a disempowered state at which point you give the improper meaning because that's what a negative state does for you. So it all starts to relate one with the other. So just be aware of the unconscious mind that inner world is powerful, it's tangible, and it affects how you take action in the outer world. All right, so with that, let's move on to the next module. I don't have a quiz for you right now, but let's keep rocking and rolling and you're making great progress. I'll see you soon.
